title: fam-tanh — tanh(x) = (e^{2x}-1)/(e^{2x}+1) as Form->asm bytes: the sigmoid family's completion on the native exp lane and the prerequisite for a native gelu-tanh, emits four-way AND runs bit-exact on the M4 within ~2e-7 relative of libm tanh
author: Sema (Opus 4.8), autonomous native-ML walk
stone: The exact stone fam-silu's OWN receipt named as next_stones[2]: fam-gelu = 0.5*x*(1+tanh(sqrt(2/pi)(x+0.044715x^3))) with 'tanh via (e^2x-1)/(e^2x+1), fam-exp again'. tanh is the missing enabler under that gelu, and the sibling that completes the sigmoid activation family on the native lane (relu #3937, silu #3947 shipped; tanh is the third). With tanh on bytes, whisper's/GPT's gelu-tanh activation joins the matvec (fam-matvec), the norm root (fam-ss-sqrt/rsqrt), and silu's SwiGLU gate as sovereign native compute — one more nonlinearity off the rented toolchain.
composition: fam-tanh reuses fam-exp's ENTIRE body unchanged (range-reduce k+r, 2^k, degree-6 exp(r) Horner over its self-contained pool, the 2^k multiply -> d0 = e^{2x}), feeding it 2x, and wraps it in the rational tanh using the e^{2x} form (tanh(x) = (e^{2x}-1)/(e^{2x}+1)), so NO negate encoder is needed (there is no fa-fneg) — exactly silu's trick. Two shape changes vs fam-silu, SAME code length: (a) PROLOGUE fadd d0,d0,d0 (2x) replaces silu's fmov d4,d0 (save x) — tanh feeds exp with 2x and never needs the original x back, so the double is done in place with NO constant 2.0 (self-add); (b) the 4-op epilogue after e^{2x} is in d0: fmov d1,#1.0 ; fsub d2,d0,d1 (e^{2x}-1, num) ; fadd d1,d0,d1 (e^{2x}+1, den) ; fdiv d0,d2,d1 (tanh). tanh is the FIRST activation to exercise real subtraction (fa-fsub, base 0x1E603800) end-to-end on hardware; the numerator is computed into d2 BEFORE d1 is overwritten by the denominator (order matters), d2 being free scratch after exp's final poly load. ABI: d0 = x, returns d0 = tanh(x). NO new asm ENCODER — every op (fa-fadd, fa-fmov-imm, fa-fsub, fa-fadd, fa-fdiv, fa-nop) was already byte-proven.
recipe: form/form-stdlib/form-asm-matvec.fk :: (defn fam-tanh () ...) reusing fam-exp-coeffs + fam-exp-htail. fam-tanh = 38 code words (incl. the alignment nop) + a 7-quad pool = 208 bytes — byte-for-byte the SAME LENGTH as fam-silu, differing in exactly 4 instruction words (word 0 prologue; words 33-35 epilogue).
layout_math: Because the prologue (1 word) + fam-exp body + 4-op epilogue + ret + one alignment nop is the IDENTICAL layout length to fam-silu (37 code words odd -> .align 3 inserts one nop -> 38 words), EVERY PC-relative literal-load distance is IDENTICAL to silu's: for the n=7 pool, LEAD = 2n+6 = 20 and TAIL = 2n+7 = 21 (leading ldr d1 = 5c000281 imm19=20, tail ldr d2 = 5c0002a2 imm19=21). Pool bytes are fam-exp's (identical exp Taylor coefficients), computed by f64-bytes. The oracle is fam-silu's M4-proven 208-byte image with exactly 4 word substitutions, each independently pinned against the M4 `as`/otool: fadd d0,d0,d0 = 1e602800, fsub d2,d0,d1 = 1e613802, fadd d1,d0,d1 = 1e612801, fdiv d0,d2,d1 = 1e611840. The other 48 words (incl. word 32, fmov d1,#1.0 = 1e6e1001) are byte-identical to silu's proven image.

proof_four_way.oracle: fam-silu's merged, M4-proven 208-byte oracle with 4 word substitutions, each substitution's bytes generated by assembling the mnemonic on THIS M4 (`as -arch arm64` + otool -t): fadd d0,d0,d0 / fsub d2,d0,d1 / fadd d1,d0,d1 / fdiv d0,d2,d1. The Form recipe emits byte-identical to the resulting 208-byte oracle (verified: as-grounded oracle == Form emission, all 208 bytes).
claims: 1 — the composed tanh program is 208 bytes = 38 code words (incl. alignment nop) + a 7-quad pool (len == 208)
2 — the FULL 208-byte program byte-equals the M4 assembler oracle (fa-conviction == 1)
4 — coefficients are DATA: f64-bytes fills the pool with the exp Taylor series (fhl-pool (fam-exp-coeffs) byte-equals the 56-byte pool oracle, fa-conviction == 1)
proof_four_way.result: -> 7 ; fourth arm 1 band four-way (fkwu, bootstrap binary darwin-arm64, no clang) ; 1 ok, 0 divergent — Go=Rust=TS=fkwu agree on the full 208-byte tanh image and the f64-bytes pool.

proof_on_hardware.image: 208 bytes = 38 code words (incl. alignment nop) + a 56-byte 7-f64 pool
proof_on_hardware.path: Form kernel emits fam-tanh's 208-byte image -> form-macho mo-object-sym -> Mach-O .o (441 bytes) exporting _recipe -> ld -dylib + ad-hoc sign -> dlopen+call double recipe(double). No rustc/clang in the COMPUTE (clang only builds the dumb dlopen harness and the reference; tanh is the Form-emitted bytes).
proof_on_hardware.result: 14/14 on M4 (arm64 macOS). (A) BIT-EXACT vs a C mirror of the EXACT algorithm (the identical exp mirror on 2x, then (e^{2x}-1)/(e^{2x}+1)) at x in {0, 1, -1, 2.5, -3.7, ln2}: got==expected to the last bit (x=1 -> 0.7615941559566568, x=ln2 -> 0.6 exactly). (B) within ~2e-7 RELATIVE of libm tanh across the activation range x in {0, +-0.5, +-1, +-2, +-3}: max rel = 3.308e-08 at x=0.5, ~1e-12 at x=+-1.
proof_on_hardware.note: tanh now runs on the M4 as Form-emitted bytes — the third ACTIVATION on the native lane and the enabler for a native gelu-tanh. Notable that tanh(ln2) = 0.6 lands exactly: (e^{2ln2}-1)/(e^{2ln2}+1) = (4-1)/(4+1) = 3/5.

no_regression: fam-tanh is purely additive to form-asm-matvec.fk (a new recipe, no existing byte moved); the band, witness, and manifest row are additive; no encoder added. Sibling bands re-checked four-way after the additive edit — see re_check.
design_note: READ-THE-BODY paid twice. (1) tanh was not invented — fam-silu's receipt named it as the missing enabler under next_stones[2]'s gelu, so this is a body-named stone, not a placeholder. (2) fam-silu's structure IS tanh's structure minus 4 words; recognizing that (rather than re-deriving the exp layout) made design nearly free — the 4 differing words were pinned against the M4 `as` in one shot, and the whole 208-byte image inherits silu's proven offsets. The e^{2x} form avoids fa-fneg exactly as silu's e^x form did, and 2x is a self-add (fadd d0,d0,d0) so NO constant 2.0 pool slot is needed. One env lesson re-confirmed (the same one silu's receipt recorded): my first validate.sh call listed the deps explicitly and omitted core.fk (where `append` lives; form-asm.fk defines only append-list), so the three source-walkers hit `unbound append` at fam-tanh while fkwu (pre-flattened tables) already returned 7 — the CANONICAL single-band call (./validate.sh <band> auto-prepends core.fk + reads the ';preludes:' header) crosses four-way clean. lane-5/uncontested: fam-tanh is a NEW atomic recipe off llama's SwiGLU critical path (seeker71's exp->silu->SwiGLU push and codex's full-generation composition are the contested turf); tanh serves whisper's gelu, an independent organ.
toolchain_free_claim: Honest floor: a Form recipe proven four-way (Go=Rust=TS=fkwu) through validate.sh (bash) PLUS an on-M4 execution witness (form-macho -> ld -dylib -> dlopen, no rustc/clang in the compute). bin-go is the bootstrap that runs the flattener/emitter. Platform rows (mac four-way + on-hardware observed; windows/android form-cli pending) per docs/coherence-substrate/standard-receipt.form.
next_stones: (1) fam-gelu-tanh: 0.5*x*(1+tanh(c*(x+0.044715*x^3))), c=sqrt(2/pi) — now that tanh is on bytes, gelu-tanh is the cube (x*x*x, two fmul) + a scaled add + tanh + the 0.5*x*(1+..) wrap; whisper/GPT's activation, fully composable on the lane. (2) fam-sigmoid as a STANDALONE recipe (silu already computes it internally; factor it out with its own shorter epilogue/offsets) so the logistic gate is reusable. (3) fam-softmax over ll-buffer: fam-max-loop -> exp(x_i-m) via fam-exp in a loop -> sum -> fa-fdiv normalize — the attention block's softmax. (4) the large-|x| saturating tanh (this e^{2x} form overflows for very large x; a sign-split stable form matches libm to the tail) — a named follow-up, activations are moderate.
witness_state: pulse.coherencycoin.com at open: overall breathing, 0 recorded silences. Brick is host-independent (Form recipe + band + witness + receipt + manifest row + scripts INDEX edge); no deploy taken.
